description: §23.5.3 — every token of the CLOSED v1 vocabulary in one `capabilities=[…]` list: the five arg-bearing caps with string-literal args (`network`, `fs-read`, `fs-write`, `spawn`, `env`) plus the presence-only boolean `db`. All six are recognized (E-FOREIGN-CAPABILITY-UNKNOWN does not fire), the hyphenated `fs-read`/`fs-write` are parsed as capability KEYWORDS (not two idents), and none of the token names resolve as scope refs.
rationale: §23.5.3: the v1 vocabulary is exactly { network, fs-read, fs-write, spawn, env, db }. A list using all six with well-formed args is valid — no E-FOREIGN-CAPABILITY-UNKNOWN, no E-SCOPE-001, no W-ATTR-001.
